title: Notopleura uberta
taxonomy: Notopleura uberta is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Notopleura, which is a part of the order Gentianales. The family Rubiaceae is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the madder family. The order Gentianales is a group of flowering plants that also includes the families Apocynaceae and Loganiaceae. These classifications are based on the plant's morphological and molecular characteristics.
uses: Notopleura uberta is a plant species used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Amazon region. Its bark, leaves, and roots are commonly used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues. The plant's extracts are believed to possess anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, making it a potential treatment for infections and wounds. Additionally, Notopleura uberta is also used to treat respiratory problems, such as coughs and bronchitis. Further research is needed to confirm the efficacy and safety of using Notopleura uberta as a medicinal plant.
